high.claim: spec claims Linear two-way sync — webhook triggers builds AND state updates flow back to Linear
high.reality: app/server/webhook.py implements only the inbound direction: HMAC verification, parse_linear_event(), and linear_issue_to_brief(). There are zero outbound Linear API calls (no issueUpdate GraphQL mutation, no linear_update_issue helper) anywhere in the provided Python source. The MCP server (mcp/pi-ceo-server.js) is referenced in CLAUDE.md for Linear writes, but that is a Node.js sidecar, not the Python backend — meaning the Python pipeline never writes state back to Linear directly.
high.recommendation: Add a Python helper (e.g. routes/webhooks.py or a linear_client.py) that POSTs the issueUpdate GraphQL mutation with Authorization: lin_api_... after a build completes, and wire it into the session completion handler in routes/sessions.py.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: The tests/ directory contains only unit tests (test_auth.py, test_webhook.py) covering auth and webhook signature verification. There are zero integration or E2E tests for the full build pipeline (sessions, evaluator, push). The 22-check 'smoke test' lives in scripts/smoke_test.py and requires a live running server plus CLI flags (--url, --password) — it is never executed by pytest and is absent from the standard test gate (python -m pytest tests/ -x -q).
high.recommendation: Either add a pytest-based integration test module under tests/test_pipeline_integration.py that stubs the Claude SDK and exercises the sessions→evaluator→push flow, or explicitly note in the spec that the 22-check suite is a manual/CI-stage smoke test requiring a live server, not a unit-test-suite claim.

high.claim: spec implies build logs stream to browser in real time via SSE (dashboard/hooks/useSSE.ts with exponential-backoff reconnect)
high.reality: The backend exposes a WebSocket endpoint (WS /ws/build/{sid}) in routes/utils.py for real-time log delivery. No distinct SSE endpoint piping session.output_lines exists in the provided server code. If useSSE.ts attempts to connect to a build-log SSE URL, it will fail silently and the client falls back to 4-second polling of /api/sessions — meaning live log streaming is not functional for the build view.
high.recommendation: Either add a GET /api/sessions/{sid}/stream SSE endpoint in routes/utils.py that yields session.output_lines as text/event-stream, or update dashboard/hooks/useSSE.ts to connect to the existing WebSocket endpoint so the advertised real-time streaming actually works.

low.claim: webhook triggers builds AND state updates flow back to Linear
low.reality: parse_linear_event() correctly handles both the action=update/state→started trigger and the RA-888 action=create/Urgent-High trigger, so the inbound half works. However, no code sets the Linear issue to In Progress on build start or to Done/Completed on build success — the issue state visible in Linear is never updated by the Python backend.
low.recommendation: After dispatching a build session (routes/sessions.py), call an outbound Linear issueUpdate mutation to transition the issue state to 'In Progress'; on session completion, transition to 'Done'.

low.reality: The provided source (dashboard/app/api/telegram/route.ts) defines helper functions (send, piCeoLogin, getPiCeoStatus, triggerBuild, createLinearIssue, getLinearBacklog) but the file is truncated mid-function — the actual command-dispatch switch/if-else block that routes incoming message.text to /start, /help, /build, etc. is not present in the visible code. Whether it exists beyond the truncation point cannot be confirmed from the provided excerpt.
low.recommendation: Confirm the POST handler body contains explicit command routing (e.g. text.startsWith('/start'), '/help', '/build') and add integration tests that send each command to the route and assert the expected reply.

low.reality: triggerBuild() is defined but the file is cut off at `const cookie = aw` — the function body is incomplete in the audited source, so it is unknown whether the /build command path (parse repo URL from text, call triggerBuild, reply with session ID) is actually wired end-to-end.
low.recommendation: Ensure triggerBuild() is complete and that the POST handler calls it when the incoming text matches /build <repo-url>, then surface a confirmation message back to the Telegram chat.

low.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
low.reality: scripts/smoke_test.py is truncated in the provided source — the _req helper function is cut off mid-body and the actual 22 check definitions are not visible. The claimed check count cannot be verified from available code.
low.recommendation: Read the full smoke_test.py and count actual check() / assert calls to confirm the 22-check claim is accurate before marking coverage complete.

low.claim: Budget tracker is instantiated but not wired — SDK metric rows capture output_len but not tokens_used
low.reality: The spec itself acknowledges this gap, but the provided test files (test_auth.py, test_webhook.py) contain no test asserting BudgetTracker behaviour or SDK metric token capture, meaning the gap will not be caught by CI regression.
low.recommendation: Add a unit test for BudgetTracker.tick() and a mock-SDK test asserting that tokens_used is populated in the metric row when the SDK returns a structured response.

low.claim: op:// refs in .env not resolved by dotenv — Pydantic validator must detect and return None
low.reality: The CLAUDE.md documents this as a required pattern (field_validator(mode='before') returning None for op:// strings), but neither sessions.py nor main.py import or reference any such validator; the implementation must exist elsewhere (e.g. config.py) but is not visible in the provided files, so conformance cannot be confirmed.
low.recommendation: Verify that a Pydantic field_validator guarding op:// strings exists in config.py or models.py and add a unit test that asserts the validator returns None for an 'op://...' input so the contract is regression-protected.

low.claim: BudgetTracker in src/tao/budget/tracker.py is a correct implementation (spec marks it complete)
low.reality: The spec itself acknowledges the tracker is instantiated but not wired into session_sdk.py — SDK metric rows capture output_len but not tokens_used. The re-export in sessions.py does not include BudgetTracker, confirming it is unreachable from the session pipeline.
low.recommendation: Instantiate BudgetTracker inside _run_claude_via_sdk() in session_sdk.py and feed it the structured SDK response's token counts so the tracker actually accumulates spend rather than sitting idle.
